name: North Yuan
note: After the Ming drove the Yuan from China (1368), the Mongol khans retreated to the steppe and continued to claim the title of Great Khan of the Mongol Empire. Periodic reunifications under strong khans (Dayan Khan, Altan Khan) challenged Ming China. Tibetan Buddhism progressively replaced shamanism/Tengriism. Formally ended when the Qing incorporated most Mongol tribes (1635-1691).

figures.name: Dayan Khan
figures.significance: Reunified the eastern Mongols after a century of fragmentation; reorganised the Mongol tribes into six tumens; his descendants ruled the Mongols for generations

figures.name: Altan Khan
figures.significance: Built Hohhot; established formal relations with Tibet — invited the Dalai Lama title and Tibetan Buddhism; the decisive conversion of Mongols to Buddhism
